perturbation.original_text: Marketing Affiliate shall be responsible for collecting all amounts due directly from the Consumer and shall bear sole responsibility for non-payment of any fees charged to the Consumer. Marketing Affiliate shall pay to Equidata, as compensation for its providing of Services under this agreement, such amounts as outlined and detailed in Exhibit A attached hereto. Such amounts shall be billed on a bi-monthly basis by Equidata and are due and payable in full by Marketing Affiliate 30 days from the invoice date.
perturbation.changed_text: Marketing Affiliate shall be responsible for collecting all amounts due directly from the Consumer and shall bear shared responsibility for non-payment of any fees charged to the Consumer. Marketing Affiliate shall pay to Equidata, as consideration for its providing of Services under this agreement, such amounts as outlined and detailed in Exhibit A attached hereto. Such amounts shall be billed on a bi-monthly basis by Equidata and are due and payable in full by Marketing Affiliate 60 days from the invoice date.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'sole responsibility' to 'shared responsibility' creates a contradiction regarding liability for non-payment, as 'shared' is undefined. Changing 'compensation' to 'consideration' introduces ambiguity, as 'consideration' has a specific legal meaning that might not align with the intended economic exchange, causing uncertainty. Also, the payment due date was changed from '30' to '60' days, creating conflict. The parties now have two terms which they must keep in mind when dealing with the payment, 'sole' and 'shared' responsibility.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Compensation. Marketing Affiliate shall be responsible for collecting all amounts due directly from the Consumer and shall bear shared responsibility for non-payment of any fees charged to the Consumer. Marketing Affiliate shall pay to Equidata, as consideration for its providing of Services under this agreement, such amounts as outlined and detailed in Exhibit A attached hereto. Such amounts shall be billed on a bi-monthly basis by Equidata and are due and payable in full by Marketing Affiliate 60 days from the invoice date. The prices set forth in Exhibit A do not include regulatory fees, sales tax, excise tax or any other fees or taxes that may be charged by states or local taxing authorities nor does it include additional fees or surcharges, including specific area Affiliate charges that may be accessed by the Credit Reporting Agencies (CRA's). Said amounts charged to Equidata will be billed separately to Marketing Affiliate and are due immediately upon receipt. Marketing Affiliate agrees to reimburse Equidata all costs of collecting any past due amounts from Marketing Affiliate by reason of non payment, including reasonable attorney fees and disbursements. Equidata reserves the right to increase the base cost of Services. Notice will be given to Marketing Affiliate in writing no less than 30 days prior to such increase taking affect. A development fee, yet To Be Determined and outlined in Exhibit A, is due upon a signed agreement of project scope. Marketing Affiliate agrees to pay promptly and in full all charges incurred through services rendered when billed. When paying by credit card, the Marketing Affiliate agrees to pay for all items that are revoked or disputed by the credit card company or the card holder along with any charges or fees charged by the credit card company including fees associated with processing the credit card transaction and that the Marketing Affiliate will be billed for those items in accordance with Equidata standard practices. Marketing Affiliate and the undersigned principal, partner or owner further agree that this Agreement will serve as a personal guaranty by the undersigned principal, partner or owner of the company, and the undersigned principal, partner or owner will become responsible for any unpaid balance past due on any invoice. The Marketing Affiliate agrees to pay a late charge of 1 l/2% per month on the unpaid, past-due amount as well as a returned check fee of not less than $35.00 per returned item. In addition, the Marketing Affiliate agrees to pay 25% attorney's fees plus court cost in the event that the Marketing Affiliate's account is referred to an attorney for collection.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'sole responsibility' to 'shared responsibility' for non-payment. This directly contradicts the contradicted text's initial statement of 'shared responsibility' and further obligations based on this responsibility. The change also alters the payment due date from 30 to 60 days, which directly conflicts with the original 30-day requirement, creating a clear inconsistency. Also, the change from 'compensation' to 'consideration' introduces ambiguity as to the meaning of the term.

perturbation.original_text: In the case of disputed charge, defined as a non-payment of an invoice for which notice of dispute has been given in writing by Marketing Affiliate to Equidata, Equidata or Marketing Affiliate may choose arbitration and Marketing Affiliate and Equidata shall be obligated by the terms agreed upon by arbitration and all monies determined owed shall be considered due and payable immediately.
perturbation.changed_text: In the case of payment dispute, defined as a disagreement regarding an invoice for which notice of disagreement has been given by Marketing Affiliate to Equidata, Equidata or Marketing Affiliate may choose arbitration and Marketing Affiliate and Equidata shall be obligated by the terms agreed upon by arbitration and all monies determined owed shall be considered due and payable within 90 days.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'disputed charge' to 'payment dispute' and 'notice of dispute' to 'notice of disagreement' creates ambiguity as to what constitutes a valid basis for initiating arbitration. Also, changed 'immediately' to '90 days'. These terms can lead to confusion regarding payment schedules.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disputes. In the case of payment dispute, defined as a disagreement regarding an invoice for which notice of disagreement has been given by Marketing Affiliate to Equidata, Equidata or Marketing Affiliate may choose arbitration and Marketing Affiliate and Equidata shall be obligated by the terms agreed upon by arbitration and all monies determined owed shall be considered due and payable within 90 days. Such arbitration does not relieve Marketing Affiliate from its obligation to promptly pay for undisputed charges in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. Such disputes shall be settled by arbitration in the City of Newport News, Virginia. Marketing Affiliate shall give Equidata written demand of dispute within 10 days of the due date of the invoice. The demand shall set forth a statement for the nature of the dispute and the amount involved. If Equidata and Marketing Affiliate can not resolve the dispute on their own within 10 days after Equidata receive said dispute, the parties shall jointly select an arbitrator. Initials: /s/ IP Source: STEEL VAULT CORP, 10-K, 12/24/2008 If the parties do not agree on the selection of an arbitrator, each party will select an arbitrator of their choosing, and the two arbitrators will jointly select a third arbitrator(s). Not later than 5 calendar days after the arbitrator(s) have been selected, the arbitrator(s) shall schedule the arbitration hearing to commence on a mutually convenient date. The hearing shall commence no later than 25 calendar days after Equidata receives receipt of dispute from Marketing Affiliate and shall continue from day to day until completed. The arbitrator(s) shall issue an award in writing no later than 10 calendar days after the conclusion of the hearing. The arbitration award shall be final and binding on both parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the payment timeline from 'immediately' to 'within 90 days' for disputed charges. The contradicted text specifies that arbitration does not relieve Marketing Affiliate from its obligation to promptly pay for undisputed charges. Also, the original text says disputed charge, whereas changed text says payment dispute, which creates ambiguity as to what exactly it means. Therefore, the altered payment timeline and the term change in the changed text directly contradicts the requirement for prompt payment of undisputed charges in the contradicted text. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as per the Misaligned Terminology definition.

perturbation.original_text: Marketing Affiliate shall not directly or indirectly solicit an existing business customer of Equidata during the term and condition of this Agreement other than for joint marketing purposes.
perturbation.changed_text: Marketing Affiliate shall not directly or indirectly contact an existing business partner of Equidata during the term and condition of this Agreement other than for collaborative promotional campaigns.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'solicit an existing business customer' to 'contact an existing business partner' and 'joint marketing purposes' to 'collaborative promotional campaigns' creates ambiguity regarding the scope of prohibited activities and exceptions, increasing the risk of disputes. This is because the term 'contact' is a broad term and 'business partner' is vague, compared to 'customer' and the act of 'soliciting'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-solicitation of Clients. Marketing Affiliate shall not directly or indirectly contact an existing business partner of Equidata during the term and condition of this Agreement other than for collaborative promotional campaigns. Further, Marketing Affiliate shall not market similar products from competing companies on any Web Site Landing Page containing the Equidata or Marketing Affiliate Web link as long as this Agreement is in effect.
perturbation.justification: While the change from 'solicit an existing business customer' to 'contact an existing business partner' introduces ambiguity, and 'joint marketing purposes' to 'collaborative promotional campaigns' makes the scope of activities broader, it does not create a *direct* contradiction with the contradicted text. The core obligation of not contacting business partners for non-collaborative promotional campaigns remains consistent. The change introduces vagueness, potentially leading to disputes, but does not create an irreconcilable conflict on the face of the text. It is possible to interpret both sections as consistent, even if the new wording presents challenges to enforcement.
